task_completion.description: The dashboard is named 'Dependency Bottleneck Correlation'
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the text 'Dependency Bottleneck Correlation' in the dashboard title area of the Graphite UI.

task_completion.description: A graph titled 'Database vs Load' is created with the correct metrics
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a graph panel titled 'Database vs Load'. In its legend or configuration, verify the presence of 'Load Z-Score' (which should be a red line) and 'DB CPU Z-Score'.

task_completion.description: A graph titled 'Web Speed vs Load' is created with the correct metrics
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a graph panel titled 'Web Speed vs Load'. In its legend or configuration, verify the presence of 'Load Z-Score' (which should be a red line) and 'Web Speed Z-Score'.

task_completion.description: A graph titled 'EC2 vs Load' is created with the correct metrics
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a graph panel titled 'EC2 vs Load'. In its legend or configuration, verify the presence of 'Load Z-Score' (which should be a red line) and 'EC2 CPU Z-Score'.

Look for visual indicators that the dashboard has been saved, such as a 'Dashboard saved' notification, the save button appearing inactive/successful, or the dashboard being viewed in the main dashboard list.

integrity.description: The agent used the Graphite web interface to create the dashboard
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show the Graphite web interface being used to configure the graphs and dashboard, rather than terminal commands.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the GUI by editing JSON files directly
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should not be seen editing a JSON file in a text editor or terminal to create the dashboard.

integrity.description: The graphs display actual plotted data
integrity.visual_evidence: The graphs should display actual plotted data lines, confirming that the metrics were successfully queried from the backend and not just mocked.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected dashboard name is exactly 'Dependency Bottleneck Correlation'. The three expected graph titles are 'Database vs Load', 'Web Speed vs Load', and 'EC2 vs Load'. Each graph must contain the normalized load metric (colored red, aliased as 'Load Z-Score') and its respective normalized metric (DB CPU, Web Speed, or EC2 CPU, appropriately aliased).
